_meta.description: Geopolitical layer — heat map scores + actor control map for all 11 verticals.

heatmap.det: OPEC+ is the original weaponized cartel but US shale has structurally weakened its leverage over Western economies. Russian crude disrupted post-2022 but absorbed via India/China routing — markets found alternatives relatively quickly. Refining is geopolitically low-risk (primarily Western-controlled). Tech race relevance is indirect — gas more important than crude for AI power. Oil is best positioned as a geopolitical HEDGE asset, not a primary geopolitical OPPORTUNITY thesis.

Russia weaponized European gas supply in 2022 — the defining resource geopolitical event of the decade. European utilities locked into 20yr US LNG SPAs that cannot be unwound. US LNG is now a direct instrument of NATO energy security policy. AI data center power demand makes NG a direct tech race input. Western policy response (LNG terminal buildout, Cheniere Stage 3, Venture Global CP2) is strong and committed.

Russian enrichment (TENEX) supplied ~28% of US SWU. Kazatomprom controls ~40% of global uranium mining (geopolitically ambiguous — between Russian and Chinese influence spheres). Prohibiting Russian Uranium Imports Act (2024) forces Western supply chain rebuild. Centrus (LEU) is the sole US enrichment company with active capacity. Nuclear AI PPA demand adds tech race urgency beyond traditional energy security framing.

Mining is relatively distributed across friendly nations (Chile, Peru, US, Australia, DRC) — no single adversarial actor controls geology. China dominates smelting (~40%) but this is a processing advantage, not a geological lock. The dominant structural constraint is the permitting asymmetry: democratic countries take 15-20yr for mine development vs. 3-5yr in authoritarian states. Tech race relevance is very high — copper is in every piece of grid infrastructure, EV, and data center.

Chile nationalization risk is real (2023 decree requiring 20-30% state participation in new projects). Chinese processing dominance: ~70% LiOH conversion, ~85% pCAM. IRA domestic content = US policy response — but with 5-10yr lag before meaningful reshoring. Cobalt (~65% DRC, Glencore dominant) and Nickel (Indonesian Class 2 oversupply) have distinct concentration profiles within this vertical.

China escalated in April 2025 to full export licensing controls on seven heavy/medium REEs (Sm, Gd, Tb, Dy, Lu, Sc, Y) plus an extraterritorial '50% rule' — products made anywhere using Chinese-origin REEs now require export licenses. Export volumes fell sharply April-May 2025, forcing production cuts at magnet-dependent OEMs. Western response has substantially upgraded: DoD-funded Lynas Seadrift TX HREE separation facility (first Western HREE capacity); MP Materials DoD 'price protection payment' floor at $110/kg NdPr + Apple $500M deal + H2 2026 commercial magnet ramp; Project Vault + executive orders; IRA EV magnet domestic content rules. Still: two companies vs. a 30-year entrenched Chinese industrial complex. HREE (Dy, Tb) Western commercial separation: still essentially zero. Pentagon lists REE as the single highest-priority critical mineral gap. Moving from 'Low' to 'Moderate' as real capital is now flowing, but the structural deficit is decades in the making.

China's Ga/Ge controls (Aug 2023) and Sb controls (Sept 2024) have created permanent market dislocations — antimony repriced from ~$12,000/tonne to ~$57,800/tonne peak (380% spike); tin and germanium structurally tighter. Western policy response upgraded from zero to nascent: DoD DPA Title III awards flowing; US EXIM approved $2.7B loan for Perpetua Resources (Stibnite — only significant Western Sb source); Project Vault ($12B public-private critical minerals reserve) includes tech metals; DoD >$80M in Stibnite project funding. However: no meaningful Western Ga/Ge primary production exists; GaN semiconductor supply chain remains structurally dependent on Chinese-processed gallium; the gap between strategic urgency and deployed capital is still the largest in the universe.

South Africa (75% Pt, ~40% Rh) has structural political/labor/Eskom fragility — not adversarially weaponized but systemically fragile. Russia (40% Pd via Norilsk) largely unavailable to Western investors post-2022 sanctions. Silver has no adversarial concentration. Platinum hydrogen economy demand is long-dated (2028-2035). Gold is the monetary/geopolitical hedge by design — central bank buying at record levels since 2022 as de-dollarization accelerates.

Water becoming geopolitical at basin level but listed investable universe does not yet directly capture weaponization. Most acute investable manifestation: Atacama water access directly constrains copper + lithium output from the world's most important mining region. DC water permits being denied in Arizona creates a new intersection between water scarcity and AI infrastructure siting.

Linde and APD are Western-controlled geopolitical ASSETS — not vulnerabilities. They are at the intersection of semiconductor fab supply chains and industrial processes with no adversarial concentration risk. Titanium supply chain actively diversifying from Russia (VSMPO-AVISMA) — ATI is the primary beneficiary. Helium supply tightening from Amur GPP stranded by sanctions benefits LIN/APD pricing power.

Russia + Belarus jointly controlled ~40% of global potash before 2022 sanctions — fertilizer was used as deliberate foreign policy. Russia imposed fertilizer export restrictions in 2022-23 as coercive economic statecraft. Morocco OCP controls ~70% of global phosphate reserves — extreme concentration. Nutrien + Mosaic are strategically critical allied-nation alternatives. Tech race relevance low — foundational food security rather than advanced technology inputs.

Air (universal); He = Qatar/US; Ti actively diversifying from Russia
